rationale: In the citing paper, Chernev and Hamilton (2009) appears only as part of a grouped citation in the managerial implications section to support a broad claim that assortment changes can affect store traffic and sales. The paper does not discuss this source's specific mechanism (option attractiveness), does not build hypotheses from it, and does not reuse its design or measures. Because the citation functions as general background evidence rather than a meaningful building block of the P2V-MAP method or argument, the role is mention-level.
